sources.author: Swyx
sources.headline: Founder at SmythOS | Previously at AWS, Microsoft, Google
sources.post_summary: Swyx discusses the evolution of AI agents, emphasizing multi-agent systems and their role in automating complex workflows beyond single-task agents. He highlights key frameworks like AutoGen and CrewAI for developers building scalable agentic applications. The post predicts agent swarms will dominate AI infrastructure in 2025.

sources.author: Andrej Karpathy
sources.headline: Founder at Eureka Labs | Ex-OpenAI, Tesla AI Director
sources.post_summary: Karpathy shares insights on training efficient AI agents for software engineering tasks, focusing on tools like Devin and their impact on developer productivity. He outlines challenges in AI infrastructure for agent reliability and long-context reasoning. Emphasizes the need for better evaluation benchmarks in agent development.

sources.author: Nat Friedman
sources.headline: Co-Founder at GitHub Copilot | Ex-Microsoft
sources.post_summary: Nat explores the convergence of AI agents and developer tools, predicting full automation of code review and deployment pipelines. He reviews recent advancements in agent orchestration platforms and their reliance on robust AI infrastructure. Calls for open-source contributions to accelerate adoption.

sources.author: Amanda Scalzi
sources.headline: AI Strategy Lead at Salesforce
sources.post_summary: Scalzi breaks down building production-grade AI agents for enterprise automation, covering vector databases and orchestration layers in AI infrastructure. She shares a case study on reducing developer toil by 70% with agentic workflows. Advises on pitfalls in scaling multi-agent systems.

sources.author: Hamel Husain
sources.headline: Independent AI Researcher | Ex-GitHub
sources.post_summary: Husain critiques current AI agent hype and proposes pragmatic developer tools for reliable automation, like improved RAG pipelines and agent scaffolding. Discusses infrastructure needs for low-latency agent inference. Provides code snippets for a minimal viable agent setup.

sources.author: Sami Rahman
sources.headline: Founder @ RevGenius | GTM Engineering Expert
sources.post_summary: Discusses building scalable GTM engines by integrating engineering with sales processes. Shares a framework for automating outbound at scale while maintaining personalization. Emphasizes data-driven iteration for revenue growth.

sources.author: Dan Disney
sources.headline: CEO @ Sales Geek | Sales Ops Leader
sources.post_summary: Outlines key sales ops metrics to track for optimizing outbound campaigns. Provides actionable tips on automation tools without losing human touch. Highlights common pitfalls in sales ops setups.

sources.author: Lindsay McKenna
sources.headline: Head of Revenue Operations @ Gong
sources.post_summary: Explains how RevOps unifies sales, marketing, and customer success for better GTM alignment. Shares case study on reducing ramp time by 30% through ops streamlining. Advocates for cross-functional content strategies.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Author & Serial Entrepreneur | GTM Strategist
sources.post_summary: Breaks down content strategy for bootstrapped SaaS GTM motions. Focuses on leveraging outbound automation ethically with value-first content. Includes templates for sales ops playbooks.

sources.author: Aasish Pappu
sources.headline: VP Revenue Operations @ Apollo.io
sources.post_summary: Details engineering a GTM motion that scales outbound to millions of touches. Covers RevOps stack recommendations and content syndication tactics. Warns against over-automation without ops foundation.

sources.author: Pieter Levels
sources.headline: Indie Maker & Solo Founder of Nomad List, Remote OK
sources.post_summary: Pieter shares his journey of shipping multiple products as a solo developer, including launching a new AI tool in under a week and achieving $10k MRR quickly. He emphasizes the power of building in public and iterating based on real user feedback. Key takeaway: Focus on velocity over perfection to validate ideas fast.

sources.author: Arvid Kahl
sources.headline: Founder of FeedbackPanda (acq. for $1M+), Author of The Embedded Entrepreneur
sources.post_summary: Arvid recounts bootstrapping and exiting his first SaaS as a solo founder, detailing the exact steps from idea to profitable product. He advises aspiring builders to start small, ship MVPs publicly, and leverage communities for growth. Highlights the importance of audience building before product launch.

sources.author: KP (Karpathy)
sources.headline: Founder of Eureka Labs, Former Director of AI at Tesla
sources.post_summary: KP discusses pivoting from academia/research to founding an AI edtech startup, sharing lessons on shipping educational AI products rapidly. He documents his building process publicly, including code releases and demos. Encourages career pivots into tech by focusing on hands-on building over credentials.

sources.author: Sahil Lavingia
sources.headline: Founder & CEO of Gumroad
sources.post_summary: Sahil reflects on his career pivot from Pinterest to solo-founding Gumroad, which powers thousands of creators. He shares tactics for shipping products as a small team and building in public to attract users and talent. Stresses resilience after pivots and the value of transparent progress updates.

sources.author: Daniel Vassallo
sources.headline: Ex-AWS, Indie Maker of Small Bets like OnePageCRM
sources.post_summary: Daniel details his pivot from Big Tech to full-time indie maker, launching multiple micro-SaaS products solo and sharing revenue updates publicly. He outlines a low-risk approach: ship fast, test markets, and pivot based on data. Ideal for those considering tech career shifts via side projects.
